classObjective: Determine whether an equation has one solution, no solution, or is an identity and apply equations to geometric situations.
workedExamples: 2-4, Example 3 — Geometry Applications (Supplementary and Complementary Angles)
2-4, Example 4 — Classify Equations (One, No, or Infinitely Many Solutions)
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: identity vs. no-solution classification; supplementary and complementary angle applications
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice classifying equation solution types and applying to geometric contexts
independentPractice: Practice problems determining solution types and solving geometry applications
assessment: Exit ticket: determine whether 3(x + 2) = 3x + 6 has one, no, or infinitely many solutions
capReflection: How can you determine whether an equation has no solution before fully solving it?
